description: §60.2/§60.9 — an endpoint that omits its `: ResponseT` fires the PRECISE E-API-RESPONSE-TYPE-UNDECLARED (parse-time, W2), NOT the catch-all E-API-ENDPOINT-MALFORMED. The response type is the parseVariant decode target (§60.5); without it the boundary-parse has nothing to decode against.
rationale: §60.2: an endpoint declaration SHALL carry `: response-type`. A well-formed head with no `: ResponseT` gets the precise E-API-RESPONSE-TYPE-UNDECLARED (the parser recovers, recording the endpoint with responseType null) — it is NOT reported by the catch-all malformed code.
